questions.question:
In a system, counting semaphore was initialized to \( 10 \), then \( 6P \)(wait) operations and \( 4V \) (signal) operations were completed on this semaphore. So _____ is the final value of the semaphore.



Operating System: NIELIT 2018

A protected variable which can be accessed and changed by particular set of operation is called \( : \)



Operating System: NIELIT 2021 Dec Scientist B

The atomic fetch-and-set \( x, y \) instruction unconditionally sets the memory location \( x \) to \( 1 \) and fetches the old value of \( x \) in \( y \) without allowing any intervening access to the memory location \( x \). Consider the following implementation of \( \text{P} \) and \( \text{V} \) functions on a binary semaphore \( \text{S} \).

void P (binary_semaphore *s) { 
    unsigned y; 
    unsigned *x = &(s->value); 
    do { 
        fetch-and-set x, y; 
    } while (y); 
}

void V (binary_semaphore *s) { 
    S->value = 0; 
}

Which one of the following is true? 



Operating System: NIELIT 2022 April Scientist B | Section B | Question: 110
options: The implementation may not work if context switching is disabled in \( \text{P} \)
Instead of using fetch-and –set, a pair of normal load/store can be used
The code does not implement a binary semaphore
questions.correct_answer: The implementation may not work if context switching is disabled in \( \text{P} \)
